title: Background tray utility; web app stays the main surface
requirement: Desktop agent is a background utility with small tray/menu-bar UI handling enrollment, detection, extraction, privacy processing, queueing, sync, updates, health, diagnostics only; all insights/dashboards stay in the web app.
evidence: No desktop app exists; web surfaces shipped (dashboard/growth/team). Plan M1.

title: Analytics Only is the default data mode
requirement: Default mode uploads derived metadata and feature signals, never raw prompt/response content.
evidence: Shipped pattern: AGENT_COLLECTION_FIELDS allowlist (src/lib/agent-collection-schema.ts); only 5 field values leave the device today.

title: No silent raw-content enablement; Full Content is future explicit opt-in
requirement: Raw prompt/response upload not in MVP; later only as explicit opt-in (Deep Coaching / Full Content) with visible disclosure and separate policy controls.
evidence: Mode enum reserved in agent; only analytics_only implemented Phase 1 (plan M3); research §5.6: richer payloads need a new founder privacy analysis.

title: No browser inspection in Phase 1
requirement: No browser history, local storage, cookies, DOM, or traffic inspection; extensions are Phase 2 at the earliest.
evidence: Matches NOT-002 tripwire; tripwire-guard blocks webextension-polyfill + extension manifests.

title: Tauri 2 + Rust core + React/TS UI + SQLite + OS credential storage
requirement: Tauri 2 app; Rust for trusted runtime logic; React/TS for onboarding/status/privacy/diagnostics; SQLite queue/state; OS keychain for secrets; signed Tauri updater.
evidence: No Rust/Tauri code in repo; packages/revealyst-agent (TS CLI) is the reference collector to port. Plan M1–M3.

title: macOS 13+ (arm64+x64) and Windows 10 22H2+ (x64); Linux deferred
requirement: Signed notarized .dmg for macOS; signed .exe/.msi for Windows; no Linux distribution in Phase 1.
evidence: No signing infra or Apple/Windows certs exist (no GitHub Environments configured). Plan M6 + founder external actions.

title: In-repo but independently released
requirement: Desktop agent lives in the monorepo with independent version, CI, release workflow, signing credentials, staged rollout, update channel; shared contracts with backend.
evidence: Precedent: packages/revealyst-agent + release-agent.yml. Adapted layout: top-level desktop-agent/ (gap analysis C5), not the spec's apps/ split.

title: Browser OAuth Authorization Code + PKCE; no in-app password form
requirement: System-browser sign-in; agent never shows a password form; deep-link callback returns a one-time code; agent exchanges code for credentials.
evidence: Better Auth has no OAuth-provider/PKCE issuance plugin configured (src/lib/auth.ts); new /api/desktop/auth/start+exchange endpoints reuse the web session + mint the existing rva1 device token. Plan M2.

title: Deep-link validation
requirement: Validate state, nonce, code lifetime, PKCE verifier, redirect source, one-time code use on revealyst://desktop-auth/callback.
evidence: Tauri deep-link plugin (new); server-side one-time-code store (new small table). Plan M2 + security tests §26.4.

title: Tokens only in OS secure storage
requirement: Refresh-token-equivalent stored in macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager; never in frontend storage, SQLite, config files, logs, crash reports.
evidence: Client-side new (keyring crate); server storage already envelope-encrypted (src/lib/credentials.ts).

title: Short-lived access + rotating refresh tokens
requirement: Spec mandates short-lived access tokens and rotating refresh tokens.
evidence: Phase 1 keeps the prod-proven static rva1 device token (rotate on re-mint, pause=revoke) — deviation recorded as D-DA-4; rotation is an M7 hardening item.

title: Device enrollment identity
requirement: Locally generated installationId; server-issued deviceId; device keypair at enrollment; never derive identity from hardware serials.
evidence: A device today = a connections row (authKind=device_token); enrollment endpoint + per-device metadata are new. Plan M2; device-key request signatures deferred with D-DA-4.

title: Multiple devices per user merge into one timeline
requirement: One user enrolls many devices; cloud merges all device events into one user timeline.
evidence: Multiple connections rows anticipated (src/db/schema/connections.ts comment); merge = same person via identity resolution; dual-source/day-MAX semantics from P0 fix (rowsForSubjects) apply — verified in plan M4 tests.

title: Device list/rename/revoke UI + heartbeat
requirement: Settings surface listing enrolled devices with rename/revoke; device heartbeat endpoint.
evidence: poll_heartbeats is org-level (not per-device); revoke path exists (pause connection → 403). Plan M2/M4.

title: Attribution to the Revealyst user; provider identity secondary
requirement: Activity attributed to the authenticated Revealyst user + installation; shared provider logins on different devices stay separately attributed.
evidence: Device token binds org+connection; person attribution ladder (src/contracts/attribution.ts); acceptance §27.5 satisfied structurally.

title: Ambiguous shared-session marking
requirement: Same computer + OS account + Revealyst session shared by multiple people → mark ambiguous, never guess.
evidence: No equivalent today; maps to declared-shared-device flag → account-level attribution or honesty gap. Plan M5; needs contract mapping in the M5 ADR.

title: Rust SourceConnector trait with detect/permissions/checkpoint/collect/health/disconnect
requirement: Connector runtime with explicit states incl. unsupported_version fallback (never partial-parse an unknown format).
evidence: New Rust; state machine mirrors the CLI's honesty-gap discipline. Plan M3/M5.

title: Claude Code connector against validated local surfaces only
requirement: Implement only against confirmed local data surfaces; unsupported format → unsupported_version.
evidence: Reference: packages/revealyst-agent/src/parse.ts (allowlist projection, fixtures under packages/revealyst-agent/tests). Rust port + fixture parity. Plan M5.

title: Claude export importer (hardened)
requirement: User-initiated import: validate archive, reject path traversal, enforce count/size limits, parse locally, convert to privacy mode, queue, delete temp files, show counts; raw conversation text never uploaded in Analytics Only.
evidence: Nothing similar exists (drag-drop log upload categorically rejected — Spec V4 §10; importer is agent-local, satisfying the no-raw-upload kill). Plan M5 + security tests.

title: Process presence disabled by default
requirement: Process presence only as coverage diagnostics, never capability evidence; default off.
evidence: Not built in Phase 1 (research R2 rejected process/app monitoring); listed Future.

title: Prompt-like content only in-process; discarded before queue persistence
requirement: Feature extractors may access prompt-like content only inside the local process; in Analytics Only it must be discarded before persistence.
evidence: Shape-only reads shipped (content_block_type sent:false). Reading prompt TEXT on-device is D-DA-5 (gap analysis C8); M3 ships shape+counts only.

title: Bounded feature output (LocalPromptFeatures)
requirement: Task category / workflow type / complexity band / prompt-structure booleans emitted as bounded enums+numbers with classifier version+confidence.
evidence: New CANONICAL_METRICS keys + allowlist rows = frozen-contract ADR (gap C9); classifier over prompt text gated on D-DA-5.

title: Deterministic local heuristics; never a cloud LLM call
requirement: Classification is deterministic, transparent, local; no cloud LLM calls from the agent in Analytics Only mode.
evidence: Constraint honored by design; no LLM dependency in the agent. CI-testable (dependency audit).

title: Event schema & payload contract
requirement: Normalized activity events with contentMode + privacy flags; Analytics Only payload allowlist + prohibited-fields list.
evidence: Phase 1 keeps day-aggregate AgentIngestRequest (gap C3 / D-DA-3); local queue is event-granular, sync aggregates; prohibited-fields enforced by the agent's payload validator + server dim guards.

title: Encrypted SQLite for non-secret state; secrets only in OS storage
requirement: agent.db (SQLCipher): installation/connector state, checkpoints, pending events, receipts, config/policy caches, diagnostics, update state.
evidence: New Rust storage layer. Plan M3.

title: Queue-before-checkpoint; duplicates over data loss
requirement: Events commit to the queue before checkpoints advance; crash duplicates acceptable because server dedups.
evidence: Server side already idempotent (natural-key upsert + delete-window, src/db/org-scope/metrics.ts); agent-side ordering is an M3 test.

title: Retention defaults incl. never-persist raw text
requirement: Pending events 30d; receipts 30d; checkpoints until reset; diag logs 7d; temp imports deleted immediately; raw text never persisted in Analytics Only.
evidence: M3 privacy tests pin the never-persist rule.

title: At-least-once + server idempotency + batch limits + retry taxonomy
requirement: At-least-once delivery; 250-event/1MB batches; gzip; retry 408/425/429/5xx with backoff+jitter; never blind-retry 400/401/403/409/413/422.
evidence: Server idempotency shipped (content-addressed); client retry engine is new Rust but the contract exists (agentIngestRequestSchema). Plan M4.

title: Partial batch acceptance
requirement: Partial acceptance works on oversized/partially-invalid batches.
evidence: ingestAgentBatch is transactional all-or-nothing; satisfied by client-side batch splitting on 413/422 (gap C7); acceptance test asserts the real mechanism.

title: Deterministic dedup priority + fingerprint
requirement: Dedup via stable IDs then deterministic fingerprint; title/first-prompt alone never a key.
evidence: Day-aggregate natural key + delete-window restatement is the shipped dedup; OTel/desktop cross-channel handled by distinct metric keys (ADR 0039 precedent); sub-case-C ADR covers dual-source person dedup.

title: Most-restrictive policy precedence
requirement: Effective policy = min(platform hard limits, org policy, user policy, connector capability).
evidence: New agent-side policy engine. Plan M3.

title: Remote config can never broaden collection silently
requirement: Config may restrict; broadening (mode upgrades) requires explicit user/admin authorization out-of-band of config.
evidence: Plan M4 signed-config design + privacy tests §26.1; mirrors research §5.6 'new founder decision, not incremental widening'.

title: Local enforcement before queue persistence + quarantine
requirement: Privacy flags contradicting active policy → quarantine, never upload (e.g. analytics_only + rawPromptIncluded:true).
evidence: New payload validator in Rust. Plan M3.

title: Signed remote config with expiry + restrictive defaults
requirement: Ed25519-signed config (version, expiry, min agent version, connector enablement, intervals, channel); on invalid signature keep last valid unexpired; else restrictive built-ins.
evidence: No remote-config service exists; new GET /api/desktop/config + a NEW signing key (never the credential KEK). Plan M4.

title: Signed Tauri updater + channels + staged rollout + halt
requirement: Official Tauri updater; internal/beta/stable channels; deterministic cohort hash(installationId+releaseId) 5→25→50→100%; halt + mandatory-update support; queue survives updates.
evidence: Nothing exists; first GitHub-Release artifact workflow in the repo. Plan M6.

title: Tray menu + onboarding + status + privacy screens
requirement: Tray menu (status/last sync/open/pause/updates/diagnostics/quit); 5-screen onboarding; status screen incl. coverage limitations; privacy screen incl. what leaves/never leaves the device, pause, delete pending, disconnect.
evidence: New React UI; 'what leaves the device' MUST render from the allowlist bridge (never hand-written copy — W3-N). Plan M1/M3/M5.

title: Honest unsupported-source disclosure
requirement: Claude Desktop shown as installed but 'detailed conversation sync is not available in Phase 1'; never claim complete Claude Desktop coverage.
evidence: scope-claims registry pattern exists (src/connectors/scope-claims.ts); add desktop entries. Plan M5.

title: Agent state model with precedence
requirement: 10-state AgentState with fixed precedence; healthy ≠ all activity captured.
evidence: New agent-side. Plan M1/M4.

title: Performance targets measured before stable
requirement: <40MB installed; <80MB idle RAM; <0.5% idle CPU; <3% active CPU; <3s to tray; <500ms warm window; <1MB/day idle network — measured in CI or internal beta.
evidence: Plan M7; perf harness new.

title: Mandatory security controls
requirement: Signed binaries/updates/config; notarization; encrypted queue; OS secret storage; strict Tauri capabilities; no unrestricted shell/fs; no remote script loading; dependency+secret scanning; tamper-resistant update verification.
evidence: Plan M1 (capabilities/CSP), M3 (queue), M4 (config), M6 (signing), CI (audits).

title: Frontend isolation + strict CSP
requirement: UI accesses fs/shell/credentials/network/db/updates only via narrowly scoped Rust commands; CSP self-only, no remote scripts.
evidence: Tauri capability files + CSP in tauri.conf.json; capability audit in desktop CI. Plan M1.

title: Secret-free logs + explicit-action diagnostic bundle
requirement: Logs carry no prompt/response/tokens/keys/cookies/file/clipboard content; bundle requires explicit user action and excludes activity payloads by default.
evidence: New POST /api/desktop/diagnostics (counts/status only, invariant b). Plan M4; log-scrub tests M3.

title: Backend API surface
requirement: Auth start/exchange/refresh/revoke; device CRUD+revoke+heartbeat; ingestion batches+capabilities; config/policy; update manifest; diagnostics.
evidence: Mapped onto existing routes where live (ingest = /api/agent/ingest; revoke = pause) + new /api/desktop/* endpoints (gap C6). Plan M2/M4/M6.

title: Desktop PR checks without signing secrets
requirement: fmt/clippy/cargo test; TS lint/typecheck/tests; connector fixtures; schema-compat; privacy-mode + Analytics Only payload tests; Tauri capability audit; dependency+secret scan; unsigned macOS+Windows builds — PR workflows never see signing secrets.
evidence: New desktop-ci.yml, path-filtered; root CI untouched except tsconfig exclude. Plan M0/M1.

title: Protected release workflow
requirement: Tag → test → build → sign → notarize → verify → checksums → signed update manifest → internal → beta → staged stable.
evidence: New release-desktop.yml with a protected GitHub Environment (first in repo); founder must create env + certs (external, fire ASAP — rule 5). Plan M6.

title: Privacy/sync/platform/security test matrix
requirement: Full §26 matrix incl. malicious deep link, PKCE mismatch, replay, tampered manifest/config, path traversal, db tampering, command authorization, injection.
evidence: Plan M2–M7; privacy tests are merge-blocking from M3 onward.

title: Phase-1 definition of done
requirement: All §27 acceptance criteria + §30 definition-of-done items hold before stable rollout.
evidence: Plan §3 exit gates map 1:1; founder-judged evidence pack via /gate-check convention.

title: Sub-case-C ADR before any Team-org desktop surface
requirement: Desktop data from an opted-in Team-org member feeds only that person's private self-view until an ADR lands exclusion-audit predicate + dual-source dedup + surfaced-not-billed.
evidence: ADR does not exist (0001–0043 checked). Gap C2 / D-DA-2; Personal-org slice ships first.

title: Resident-collector go/no-go
requirement: SYNC-007/TEL-017 demoted the resident collector pending cadence telemetry; building the Desktop Agent requires an explicit founder green-light.
evidence: Gap C1 / D-DA-1; no binary ships before sign-off; only the M0–M1 non-collecting foundation may proceed (repo enablement + app shell).

title: Phase-1 scope inside every existing tripwire
requirement: No extension/proxy, no MDM, no raw log upload, no keystroke/clipboard/screen capture, no network interception, no provider credentials, content-free by shape.
evidence: Spec §2.3/§29 match NOT-002/003/009 + PRIV-008 + research R2 verbatim; tripwire-guard.mjs patterns unaffected by Tauri deps (verified).
